quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in place by beating their wings up to 80 times per second. These tiny acrobats need to eat about half their body weight in nectar each day to fuel their incredible flying abilities.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Pacific Northwest
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are common visitors to the Strait of Juan de Fuca, but they have some surprising habits most people don't know about. Learn why these charming marine mammals are such successful survivors in local waters.

medium_read.title: How Gardens Help Us Age with Joy
medium_read.teaser: Scientific research shows that gardening offers remarkable benefits for mind and body as we grow older. From improving memory to reducing stress, tending plants may be one of nature's best medicines.

long_read.title: The Ancient Art of Hungarian Embroidery
long_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women have created breathtaking embroidered textiles that tell stories of their villages and families. This beautiful tradition connects generations and preserves cultural memory through needle and thread.

port_angeles.title: Roosevelt Elk in Olympic National Park
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot Roosevelt elk in the Hoh Rain Forest and along Hurricane Ridge. These majestic animals, named after President Theodore Roosevelt, are the largest variety of elk in North America and can often be seen grazing in meadows during early morning or evening hours.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right, Barbara! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to keep those wings beating!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — faster than almost any other animal!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Those little hearts are amazing — they beat about 1,200 times per minute! That's 20 times every single second.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To power their incredible metabolism, hummingbirds have the fastest heart rate of any bird. When resting at night, their heart rate drops dramatically to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: It's 'pillangó' — such a lovely, flowing word! The other words mean bird (madár), flower (virág), and little bee (méhecske).
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' comes from an old word meaning 'fluttering' or 'dancing in the air,' which perfectly describes how butterflies move through gardens.

puzzle_3.question: What unique feature makes the Strait of Juan de Fuca an important wildlife habitat?
options: It's the warmest water in Washington
It connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound
It's the deepest strait in North America
puzzle_3.correct_response: Perfect! The strait's connection between ocean and inland waters creates a rich highway for salmon, orcas, seals, and countless other marine creatures you can see from Port Angeles!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's because the strait connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound! This makes it a vital migration route and feeding ground for whales, salmon, seals, and so many other wonderful animals.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca serves as a crucial corridor for migrating salmon and marine mammals. Its nutrient-rich waters support an incredible diversity of sea life year-round.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
